metadata.methodology: Structured Analytic Tradecraft (ACH, Indicators, Driver Analysis)

executive_summary.bluf: Operation Epic Fury shifts from kinetic ops to maritime blockade and coercive pressure; ceasefire holding but fragile

key_judgments.statement: At least one additional NATO member state (likely Poland or Estonia) will report a drone incursion or crash on its territory within the next 7 days.
China will conduct at least one military exercise or patrol near Taiwan within the next 7 days as a follow-up to Xi's warning.
Iran will conduct at least one ballistic missile test or visible deployment movement within the next 7 days to signal reconstitution of its strike capability.
The Digital Asset Market Clarity Act will pass the full Senate within 7 days, given bipartisan committee support and administration backing.
Flávio Bolsonaro will lose at least 5 percentage points in polling within 7 days due to the leaked tape scandal.

theaters.name: Operation Epic Fury
theaters.lead: The US-Israeli campaign has transitioned from high-intensity strikes to a maritime blockade strategy, with 42 vessels turned back from the Strait of Hormuz. 200 aircraft and 25 ships are now involved in blockade enforcement.
developments: Ceasefire holding on an open-ended basis but no diplomatic off-ramp defined
Admiral Cooper and CJCS Caine briefed President Trump on options including limited ground interventions and energy infrastructure strikes
Iran-aligned Iraqi militias (Islamic Resistance in Iraq coalition) have launched 400+ strikes across Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, and Jordan
Israel delivered advanced defensive weaponry to UAE for missile/drone protection
Israel on heightened alert; media reports suggest Trump nearing decision on renewed military action
Iran's decentralized power structure increasingly dominated by IRGC elements, complicating diplomatic pathways
theaters.assessment: Moderate-to-high probability of protracted standoff (65-75%). Risk of escalation to limited ground operations or energy infrastructure strikes. Absent diplomatic breakthrough, the current posture risks prolonged regional instability.

theaters.name: 2. Energy & Economic Disruption
theaters.lead: The Strait of Hormuz closure continues to impact global energy markets with no timeline for reopening under the US-proposed Maritime Freedom Construct.
developments: Brent crude at $124.67/bbl — highest since 2022 energy crisis
US proposes 'Maritime Freedom Construct' coalition for safe passage; diplomatic buy-in uncertain
Iran restates claim to Strait of Hormuz as sovereign waterway
Global shipping disruptions continue; insurance premiums for Gulf transits remain elevated
theaters.assessment: Oil prices likely to remain elevated ($110-130/bbl range) for at least 2-3 weeks. Risk of sustained $100+ pricing if blockade continues beyond May.

theaters.name: Strategic Adaptation
theaters.lead: Ukraine has used the Middle East crisis as a strategic window to deepen Gulf security partnerships.
developments: Kyiv sent anti-drone specialists to 5 Gulf states
Long-term security partnership signed with Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and UAE
Zelenskyy cites Iran conflict distraction as obstacle to Russia peace talks
Ukraine formally asked Israel to seize ship carrying grain stolen by Russia
theaters.assessment: Ukraine is effectively diversifying security relationships. The Iran crisis provides diplomatic cover for deeper Ukraine-Gulf ties that would have been politically difficult before.

theaters.name: 4. Indo-Pacific & China
theaters.lead: China conducted combat readiness patrols near Scarborough Shoal as US attention remains fixed on the Middle East.
developments: PLA patrols near Scarborough Shoal in disputed South China Sea
Foreign Minister Wang Yi warns Taiwan issue is 'biggest point of risk' for US-China relations
Trump scheduled to visit Beijing in 2 weeks with Strait of Hormuz still closed
Australia publishes 2026 National Defence Strategy emphasizing regional deterrence
theaters.assessment: China is testing US capacity for two-theater engagement. The Trump-Beijing visit will be a critical indicator of US-China tension management under current crisis conditions.
